input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.content:
Here's the full `AppShell.xaml` and its code-behind:

**AppShell.xaml**

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
       xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
       xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
       xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Views"
       FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
       Title="MyMauiApp">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — two tabs inside one flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>
```

**AppShell.xaml.cs**

```csharp
namespace MyMauiApp;

public partial class AppShell : Shell
{
    public AppShell()
    {
        InitializeComponent();

        // Register the detail page so you can navigate with:
        //   await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(
        //       $"productDetails?id={product.Id}");
        Routing.RegisterRoute("productDetails", typeof(Views.ProductDetailsPage));
    }
}
```

### Key points

| Concept | What's happening |
|---|---|
| **`FlyoutItem`** | Each one becomes a row in the flyout menu |
| **`Tab` with two `ShellContent`s** | Creates bottom tabs (Active / Archived) under Products |
| **`ContentTemplate`** | Lazy-loads each page on first visit |
| **`Routing.RegisterRoute`** | Makes `productDetails` a *global* route you can push onto any navigation stack |

To navigate from a product list:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"productDetails?id={product.Id}");
```

Receive the parameter in `ProductDetailsPage` with `[QueryProperty("Id", "id")]` or by implementing `IQueryAttributable`.

Structure needed:
1. Flyout with:
   - "Dashboard" section (single page)
   - "Products" section with two tabs: "Active" and "Archived"
   - "Settings" page
2. Route registration for ProductDetailsPage

I can just write this out directly - no need to explore a codebase.
